metadata.description: Lag-Width-Undershoot (LWU) HRF with dual Gaussian components

semantic_description.purpose: Validate the LWU (Lag-Width-Undershoot) HRF implementation with dual Gaussian mathematics
semantic_description.algorithm:
The LWU HRF models hemodynamic response using two Gaussian components: h(t; τ, σ, ρ) = exp(-(t-τ)²/(2σ²)) - ρ * exp(-(t-τ-2σ)²/(2(1.6σ)²))
Parameters: - τ (tau): Lag/time-to-peak of main component (default: 6s) - σ (sigma): Width (std dev) of main component, must be > 0.05 (default: 2.5s) - ρ (rho): Undershoot amplitude relative to main, 0 ≤ ρ ≤ 1.5 (default: 0.35)
Components: 1. Main positive Gaussian: centered at τ with width σ 2. Undershoot Gaussian: centered at τ+2σ with width 1.6σ, amplitude ρ
Normalization options: - "none": No scaling applied - "height": Scale so max absolute value = 1
Key properties: - Main peak occurs around τ seconds - Undershoot peak around τ + 2σ seconds - Characteristic biphasic shape when ρ > 0 - Smooth continuous function
semantic_description.mathematical_properties: - h(t) is continuous and smooth for all t - Main peak location approximately at τ - Undershoot minimum around τ + 2σ + small offset - For default params: peak ~6s, undershoot minimum ~11-12s - Total area depends on ρ: larger ρ means smaller net area - Function approaches zero as t → ±∞ - Maximum value occurs in main component for typical parameters
semantic_description.edge_cases: - σ ≤ 0.05 should trigger error or warning - ρ outside [0, 1.5] should trigger error or warning - ρ = 0 eliminates undershoot (single Gaussian) - ρ = 1 creates balanced positive/negative components - Very large σ creates very wide, low amplitude response

input.name: time_vector
input.description: Time points from 0 to 30 seconds with 0.1 second intervals

input.name: default_params
input.description: Default LWU parameters: tau=6, sigma=2.5, rho=0.35

input.name: edge_times
input.description: Specific time points for precise validation

check.location: seq(0, 30, by = 0.1)[which.max(hrf_lwu(seq(0, 30, by = 0.1), tau=6, sigma=2.5, rho=0.35))]
max(abs(hrf_lwu(seq(0, 30, by = 0.1), tau=6, sigma=2.5, rho=0.35, normalize="height")))
abs(hrf_lwu(5, tau=6, sigma=2.5, rho=0) - hrf_lwu(7, tau=6, sigma=2.5, rho=0))
seq(0, 30, by = 0.1)[which.max(hrf_lwu(seq(0, 30, by = 0.1), tau=8, sigma=1.5, rho=0.5))]

implementations.R:
# R reference implementation # Function: hrf_lwu(t, tau = 6, sigma = 2.5, rho = 0.35, normalize = "none") # Location: R/hrf-functions.R lines 449-484
# Test default parameters time_vec <- seq(0, 30, by = 0.1) result_default <- hrf_lwu(time_vec, tau = 6, sigma = 2.5, rho = 0.35)
# Test no undershoot result_no_undershoot <- hrf_lwu(time_vec, tau = 6, sigma = 2.5, rho = 0)
# Test custom parameters result_custom <- hrf_lwu(time_vec, tau = 8, sigma = 1.5, rho = 0.5)
# Test height normalization result_normalized <- hrf_lwu(time_vec, tau = 6, sigma = 2.5, rho = 0.35, normalize = "height")
# Test specific time points key_times <- c(0, 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 20) result_key <- hrf_lwu(key_times, tau = 6, sigma = 2.5, rho = 0.35)
implementations.Python:
# Python implementation following semantic specification
import numpy as np
def hrf_lwu(t, tau=6, sigma=2.5, rho=0.35, normalize="none"): """ Lag-Width-Undershoot (LWU) HRF with dual Gaussian components.
Args: t: Time points (scalar or array) tau: Lag/time-to-peak of main component (default: 6) sigma: Width of main component, must be > 0.05 (default: 2.5) rho: Undershoot amplitude, 0 <= rho <= 1.5 (default: 0.35) normalize: "none" or "height" (default: "none")
Returns: LWU HRF values at time points t """ # Parameter validation assert sigma > 0.05, "sigma must be > 0.05" assert 0 <= rho <= 1.5, "rho must be between 0 and 1.5" assert normalize in ["none", "height"], "normalize must be 'none' or 'height'"
# Convert to numpy array t = np.asarray(t, dtype=float)
# Main positive Gaussian component term1_exponent = -((t - tau)**2) / (2 * sigma**2) term1 = np.exp(term1_exponent)
# Undershoot Gaussian component # Peak at tau + 2*sigma, width = 1.6*sigma undershoot_center = tau + 2 * sigma undershoot_width = 1.6 * sigma term2_exponent = -((t - undershoot_center)**2) / (2 * undershoot_width**2) term2 = rho * np.exp(term2_exponent)
# Combine components response = term1 - term2
# Apply normalization if normalize == "height": max_abs_val = np.max(np.abs(response)) if max_abs_val > 1e-10: response = response / max_abs_val
return response
